refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the functional requirement (enable task execution at target productivity) from the verification criterion (measured KPI ≥ target). This clarifies that productivity is an emergent property dependent on vehicle capability, operator effectiveness (on-site or remote), task planning, and safety constraints. High safety criticality demands explicit linkage to safety compliance.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure actual productivity (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations under controlled conditions; cross-reference against worksite safety plan compliance logs and operational policy adherence records.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving operations (dig, load, transport, dump) with specified cycle time and payload capacity.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to sustain target productivity.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints (geofencing, speed limits, load limits, emergency stop) that do not degrade productivity below target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall provide task planning and scheduling capability to optimize resource utilization toward productivity target.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-payload, low-cycle-time vehicle with real-time telemetry feedback and operator control loop (on-site or remote) such that task execution rate meets productivity_target under safety constraints.

DP-B: Decompose productivity target into vehicle performance specs (payload, cycle time, fuel efficiency) and operator workload specs (situational awareness latency, control responsiveness); allocate to vehicle subsystems and operator interface subsystems independently.

DP-C: Implement a closed-loop productivity monitoring and adaptive task scheduling system that dynamically adjusts task allocation and vehicle routing to maintain productivity_target despite operational variability and safety constraints.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Decompose productivity target into vehicle performance specs (payload, cycle time, fuel efficiency) and operator workload specs (situational awareness latency, control responsiveness); allocate to vehicle subsystems and operator interface subsystems independently.

potential_sources: Vehicle cycle time and operator control latency are coupled: slow vehicle response or poor telemetry feedback can reduce effective productivity even if vehicle hardware is capable.
Safety constraint enforcement (geofencing, load limits) may couple with vehicle performance: overly restrictive safety zones or load limits could prevent reaching productivity_target.
Task scheduling and vehicle availability are coupled: if task planning does not account for vehicle maintenance windows or safety downtime, productivity target may be unachievable.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B acknowledges these couplings by explicitly allocating to both vehicle and operator subsystems. Coupling is managed through interface specifications (Telemetry latency, Tasking command format, SafetyAlert thresholds) rather than eliminated. This is appropriate for a complex system where some coupling is inherent.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Productivity_target is achievable within the physical constraints of the vehicle platform and the worksite environment (soil conditions, terrain, weather).
Assumption: Operator skill and training are sufficient to sustain target productivity; training and competency are in scope.
Risk: If vehicle cycle time cannot be reduced below a threshold (e.g., due to hydraulic response limits), productivity_target may be unachievable regardless of operator interface quality.
Risk: If teleoperation latency exceeds operator tolerance (e.g., >500 ms round-trip), remote operator productivity may fall below on-site operator productivity, creating a two-tier system.
Risk: Safety constraints (e.g., geofencing) may be so restrictive that they prevent reaching productivity_target; safety and productivity may be in conflict.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-B to explicitly state the allocation strategy: (1) Vehicle subsystem shall achieve cycle time ≤ [X] seconds and payload ≥ [Y] tons; (2) Operator interface (on-site and remote) shall provide situational awareness latency ≤ [Z] ms and control responsiveness ≤ [W] ms; (3) Safety Services shall enforce constraints that reduce productivity by ≤ [P]% relative to unconstrained operation. This makes the decomposition testable and highlights where conflicts may arise.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. It is responsible for coordinating task execution, monitoring productivity KPI, and ensuring that all subsystems operate within safety and operational constraints. The NGHE Solution owns the productivity acceptance criterion and is the primary module for this FR.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits real-time telemetry (vehicle position, payload, cycle time, fuel consumption, safety status) to external monitoring systems (Site Manager dashboard, Maintenance systems). Telemetry is used to measure productivity KPI and diagnose performance issues.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task commands from Site Manager or task planning system (e.g., 'excavate zone A, load truck B, dump at location C'). Tasking drives the productivity workflow.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits safety alerts (geofence violations, load limit exceeded, emergency stop triggered) to Safety Manager and operator. SafetyAlert is critical for maintaining safety compliance while executing productivity tasks.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to communicate with remote Tele-operator (if remote operation is active) and to receive task updates from Site Manager. Network Link is essential for teleoperation and task scheduling.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level module that owns the productivity KPI acceptance criterion. All sub-functions (vehicle operation, teleoperation, safety enforcement, task scheduling) are contained within NGHE Solution's scope. Diffusion is minimal: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary. This is the correct assignment.

Vehicle module owns the physical execution of earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) and contributes directly to cycle time and payload capacity. However, Vehicle alone cannot achieve the productivity FR because it lacks operator interface, task scheduling, and safety constraint enforcement. Assigning FR to Vehicle would create high diffusion (40%) across Teleoperation and Safety Services modules. Not recommended as primary IM.

Teleoperation module owns remote operator interface and situational awareness. It contributes to operator effectiveness and thus to productivity. However, Teleoperation does not own vehicle hardware, task scheduling, or safety enforcement. Assigning FR to Teleoperation would create high diffusion across Vehicle and Safety Services. Not recommended as primary IM.

Safety Services module owns safety constraint enforcement (geofencing, load limits, emergency stop). It is essential for meeting the safety criticality requirement but does not own vehicle operation, operator interface, or task scheduling. Assigning FR to Safety Services would create very high diffusion. Not recommended as primary IM.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary because it is the system-level integrator that coordinates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to achieve the productivity KPI. The FR is an emergent property of the integrated system, not a property of any single subsystem. NGHE Solution owns the acceptance criterion and is responsible for ensuring that all subsystems work together to meet productivity_target while maintaining safety compliance.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution is assigned the FR but does not have explicit ownership of task scheduling and resource optimization, productivity may be suboptimal. Recommend that NGHE Solution includes a task planning/scheduling subsystem or interface.

Risk: If Vehicle subsystem performance (cycle time, payload) is insufficient, NGHE Solution cannot compensate through operator interface or safety services alone. Recommend that Vehicle subsystem performance specs are derived from productivity_target and validated early.

Risk: If Safety Services constraints are too restrictive, they may prevent reaching productivity_target. Recommend that safety constraints are co-designed with productivity targets to ensure feasibility.

Risk: If Teleoperation latency is high, remote operator productivity may be significantly lower than on-site operator productivity. Recommend that latency requirements are specified and validated for both on-site and remote operation modes.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify that NGHE Solution owns the integration and coordination of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Explicitly allocate sub-FRs to each subsystem: (1) Vehicle owns FR-NGHE-001.1 (task execution with cycle time and payload); (2) Teleoperation owns FR-NGHE-001.2 (operator interface and situational awareness); (3) Safety Services owns FR-NGHE-001.3 (safety constraint enforcement); (4) NGHE Solution owns FR-NGHE-001.4 (task planning and scheduling). This makes the module responsibilities clear and enables independent verification of each subsystem.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (decompose productivity target into vehicle and operator specs) is a sound logical solution to FR-NGHE-001 (achieve productivity_target). The DP acknowledges that productivity is an emergent property of vehicle capability and operator effectiveness, and it proposes a decomposition strategy that enables independent allocation and verification. The DP is testable: vehicle cycle time and payload can be measured; operator latency and responsiveness can be measured; productivity KPI can be measured. The DP does not over-constrain the design (e.g., it does not mandate a specific vehicle architecture or teleoperation technology). Pass.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Vehicle subsystem must deliver cycle time and payload specs. If Vehicle subsystem is not properly resourced or if vehicle hardware is inadequate, the entire productivity FR fails. Recommend early validation of vehicle performance specs.

Diffusion Risk 2: Teleoperation subsystem must deliver operator interface with low latency and high responsiveness. If Teleoperation is not properly designed, remote operator productivity may be significantly lower than on-site operator productivity. Recommend latency and responsiveness requirements are specified and validated.

Diffusion Risk 3: Safety Services subsystem must enforce constraints without degrading productivity below target. If safety constraints are too restrictive, productivity_target may be unachievable. Recommend co-design of safety and productivity requirements.

Diffusion Risk 4: Task scheduling and resource optimization are critical for achieving productivity_target. If task planning is not integrated into NGHE Solution, productivity may be suboptimal. Recommend explicit allocation of task scheduling responsibility.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary because it is the system-level integrator. However, the success of this assignment depends on clear allocation of sub-FRs to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems, and on effective integration and coordination across subsystems. Recommend that integration points (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are clearly specified and validated.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Productivity KPI (m³/h or ton/h) is measurable during representative operations. Verification requires: (1) instrumentation of vehicle to measure cycle time, payload, and fuel consumption; (2) instrumentation of operator interface to measure latency and responsiveness; (3) logging of safety alerts and constraint violations; (4) measurement of actual productivity over the agreed evaluation period. Verification is feasible but requires careful test planning and instrumentation.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of FR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution is clear: NGHE Solution is responsible for achieving productivity_target while maintaining safety compliance. However, the allocation of sub-FRs to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems must be made explicit in the next decomposition level. Recommend that sub-FR allocation is documented and reviewed before proceeding to detailed design.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Refine DP-B to include explicit performance specs for Vehicle (cycle time ≤ X seconds, payload ≥ Y tons), Teleoperation (latency ≤ Z ms, responsiveness ≤ W ms), and Safety Services (constraint impact ≤ P% on productivity). This makes the DP testable and enables independent verification.

Revision 2: Clarify the allocation of sub-FRs to subsystems: Vehicle owns task execution (FR-NGHE-001.1), Teleoperation owns operator interface (FR-NGHE-001.2), Safety Services owns constraint enforcement (FR-NGHE-001.3), NGHE Solution owns task scheduling (FR-NGHE-001.4). Document this allocation in the design specification.

Revision 3: Specify the integration and coordination mechanisms across subsystems: how does NGHE Solution coordinate Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services? What are the critical interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link)? How are conflicts (e.g., safety vs. productivity) resolved? Document this in the system architecture.

Revision 4: Identify and mitigate the diffusion risks: (a) Vehicle performance validation plan; (b) Teleoperation latency and responsiveness validation plan; (c) Safety constraint feasibility analysis; (d) Task scheduling optimization strategy. Document these plans in the project schedule.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that is not atomic. It requires decomposition into sub-FRs that can be allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. The refined FR and DP-B provide a sound decomposition strategy, and NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary. However, the next level of decomposition is essential to clarify the allocation of sub-FRs to subsystems and to specify the integration and coordination mechanisms. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will enable independent verification of each subsystem and will highlight potential conflicts (e.g., safety vs. productivity) that must be resolved early.
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR identifies four sub-FRs: (1) Vehicle task execution, (2) Operator interface and situational awareness, (3) Safety constraint enforcement, (4) Task planning and scheduling. Each sub-FR should be decomposed to the next level to specify performance requirements, design parameters, and implementation modules. This will enable a clear allocation of responsibilities across subsystems and will support independent verification and integration testing.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving operations (dig, load, transport, dump) with cycle time ≤ [X] seconds and payload capacity ≥ [Y] tons, measured under representative operating conditions.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or remote) shall provide situational awareness with latency ≤ [Z] ms and control responsiveness ≤ [W] ms, enabling the operator to sustain target productivity.

FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints (geofencing, speed limits, load limits, emergency stop) such that the impact on productivity is ≤ [P]% relative to unconstrained operation.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall provide task planning and scheduling capability to optimize resource utilization and maintain productivity ≥ productivity_target over the evaluation period.

assumptions: Assumption 1: Productivity_target is achievable within the physical constraints of the vehicle platform and the worksite environment.
Assumption 2: Operator skill and training are sufficient to sustain target productivity; training and competency are in scope.
Assumption 3: Vehicle cycle time and payload capacity are the primary drivers of productivity; other factors (fuel consumption, maintenance downtime) are secondary.
Assumption 4: Teleoperation latency and responsiveness are acceptable for remote operation; if latency exceeds operator tolerance, remote operation may not be feasible.
Assumption 5: Safety constraints can be enforced without preventing the achievement of productivity_target; safety and productivity are compatible objectives.
Assumption 6: Task scheduling and resource optimization are within the scope of NGHE Solution; external task planning systems (e.g., site-level scheduling) are out of scope.
open_risks: Risk 1: Vehicle cycle time may be limited by hydraulic response time or mechanical constraints, preventing the achievement of productivity_target.
Risk 2: Teleoperation latency may exceed operator tolerance, making remote operation infeasible or significantly reducing remote operator productivity.
Risk 3: Safety constraints may be so restrictive that they prevent reaching productivity_target; safety and productivity may be in conflict.
Risk 4: Task scheduling and resource optimization may be complex and may require advanced algorithms (e.g., machine learning) to achieve productivity_target.
Risk 5: Operator training and competency may be insufficient to sustain target productivity; training requirements may be underestimated.
Risk 6: Worksite conditions (soil, terrain, weather) may vary significantly, affecting productivity; the evaluation period may not be representative of all operating conditions.
evidence_needed: Evidence 1: Vehicle performance specifications (cycle time, payload capacity) derived from productivity_target and validated against vehicle hardware capabilities.
Evidence 2: Teleoperation latency and responsiveness requirements specified and validated through testing with representative operators.
Evidence 3: Safety constraint specifications (geofencing, speed limits, load limits) co-designed with productivity requirements to ensure feasibility.
Evidence 4: Task scheduling and resource optimization strategy documented and validated through simulation or pilot testing.
Evidence 5: Operator training and competency requirements specified and validated through training and evaluation programs.
Evidence 6: Productivity measurement methodology documented, including instrumentation, data collection, and analysis procedures.
Evidence 7: Representative operating conditions defined and agreed with stakeholders (Site Manager, Safety Manager, Maintenance/Quality).
Evidence 8: Acceptance criteria for each sub-FR specified and agreed with stakeholders before detailed design begins.
